channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: How Coventry earned Premier League return after 25 years
item.description: Coventry City are back in the Premier League after 25 years. Here's how they achieved promotion under the guidance of Frank Lampard.

item.title: Carrick leaves door open for Rashford return
item.description: Michael Carrick says there has been no decision made on the Manchester United future of Marcus Rashford, who has spent the season on loan at Barcelona.

item.title: Bournemouth close to appointing Rose
item.description: Bournemouth are close to appointing Marco Rose as Andoni Iraola's replacement as head coach.

item.title: Fans being fleeced by £111 World Cup train tickets - FSA
item.description: The Football Supporters' Association says fans are being "fleeced" and "gouged" by the high cost of train tickets to World Cup games in New Jersey.

item.title: Chelsea midfielder Palmer denies Man Utd links
item.description: Chelsea midfielder Cole Palmer says he has "no plans" to leave the club amid links to Manchester United.

item.title: What does the Saudi Arabia PIF's change in approach mean for Newcastle?
item.description: Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund (PIF) set out to disrupt, but the fund has sent tremors through the world of sport in a very different way this week.

item.title: One of Arsenal's greatest unsung heroes - Keown's tribute to Manninger
item.description: Former Arsenal defender Martin Keown pays tribute to his old friend and former Gunners team-mate Alex Manninger, who has died at the age of 48.

item.title: Arsenal light training ground fire to inspire players
item.description: Arsenal light a fire at their London Colney training ground to motivate players before crucial week.

item.title: Liverpool to rival Man Utd for Wharton - Saturday's gossip
item.description: Liverpool are set to compete with Manchester United for Crystal Palace's Adam Wharton, Tottenham have agreement to sign Bournemouth's Marcos Senesi, Newcastle United are prepared to sell Yoane Wissa, plus more.

item.title: Celtic 'not good enough' for O'Neill to dream of cup glory
item.description: A revenge mission against St Mirren in the Scottish Cup semi-finals could set up Martin O'Neill for a huge farewell - but the interim Celtic manager is reluctant to look that far ahead.

item.title: Guardiola says he enjoys watching Arsenal play
item.description: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola says he enjoys watching Arsenal play as he can "learn a lot" from them.

item.title: Quiz: Name every team to have been promoted to the Premier League
item.description: Forty-one teams have been promoted into the Premier League, can you name them all?

item.title: Caicedo signs new seven-year Chelsea deal
item.description: Moises Caicedo says his "dream is to become a Chelsea legend" after signing a lucrative new contract until 2033.

item.title: Leicester need something special to beat drop - Rowett
item.description: Leicester City boss Gary Rowett says "belief" in increasingly dire times will be key to the Foxes' Championship survival hopes.

item.title: Website part-owned by Boehly offers Chelsea FA Cup tickets for £1,705
item.description: A US-based website linked to Todd Boehly is selling Chelsea tickets at inflated prices, leaving the club's fans angry.

item.title: The rollercoaster race for second spot - who's going up with Coventry?
item.description: With two weeks of the regular season left, Ipswich Town, Millwall, Southampton and Middlesbrough are chasing Premier League football.

item.title: Fernandez back to face Man Utd but Chelsea's problems remain
item.description: Enzo Fernandez returns for Chelsea this weekend, but questions remain.

item.title: Motherwell & Hearts dominate PFA Scotland Team of the Year
item.description: There are five Motherwell players in the PFA Scotland Premiership Team of the Year, with four from table-topping Hearts.

item.title: Guardiola proud to be challenging Arsenal for title
item.description: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola said the title race is "over" with six games to play if they lose to Arsenal on Sunday but there are still "many things to do".

item.title: UFC star Pimblett rants about Arsenal and Arteta
item.description: The Wayne Rooney Show welcomes UFC star Paddy Pimblett this week, and it's fair to say, he's not a huge fan of Arsenal.

item.title: Wood returns for Nottingham Forest after knee injury
item.description: Nottingham Forest striker Chris Wood says it felt like there was "no light at the end of the tunnel" during parts of his recovery.

item.title: 'I wasn't surprised' - Slot critical of overturned Liverpool penalty
item.description: Liverpool boss Arne Slot believes the penalty that was overturned by VAR in his side's Champions League second-leg defeat by PSG would "usually" have been given, but says the Reds have had bad luck with refereeing decisions this season.

item.title: Scotland score in stoppage time to salvage draw with Belgium
item.description: Watch the highlights as Scotland score in stoppage time to salvage a World Cup qualification draw with Belgium.

item.title: Sutton's predictions v boxer Molly McCann & the Boo Radleys
item.description: Chris Sutton takes on boxer Molly McCann and The Boo Radley's frontman Sice Rowbottom plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for this weekend's Premier League fixtures.

item.title: Scotland carry no burden in chase for World Cup spot
item.description: Scotland head coach Melissa Andreatta insists the squad do not feel burdened by missing out on previous major finals and have created a "perfect storm" in their quest to reach next year's World Cup in Brazil.

item.title: Nobody says who called first, but Arteta and Guardiola speak again - Balague
item.description: As Manchester City prepare to host Arsenal in Sunday's crucial Premier League meeting between the top two, European football expert Guillem Balague looks at how the two managers have evolved.

item.title: Italy made me a manager when England 'discouraged' me - Cole
item.description: Ashley Cole won 107 caps for England, seventh on the all‑time list, but says he was "discouraged" by those in the football pyramid from becoming a head coach.

item.title: All your FPL stats and Premier League team news in one place
item.description: Every key bit of Premier League team news and the most important Fantasy Premier League statistics - all in one place.

item.title: 'It's make or break for him' - is it silverware or the sack for Arteta?
item.description: Speaking on the Monday Night Club, former Premier League striker and BBC Sport pundit Chris Sutton says Arsenal head coach Mikel Arteta must win the Premier League or the Champions League this season or risk losing his job at the Emirates.

item.title: Okafor fires Leeds to first league win at Old Trafford since 1981
item.description: Leeds boost their Premier League survival hopes with a 2-1 win against 10-man Manchester United at Old Trafford.

item.title: Martinez red card 'shocking decision' - Carrick
item.description: Manchester United boss Michael Carrick says Lisandro Martinez's red card for pulling Dominic Calvert-Lewin's hair was a "shocking decision" during a 2-1 defeat by Leeds United.

item.title: Coaching or confidence - What should De Zerbi prioritise at Spurs?
item.description: Speaking on the Monday Night Club, former Premier League defender Nedum Onuoha says new Tottenham head coach Roberto de Zerbi is right to focus on raising morale rather than tactics as he looks to keep the club in the Premier League.

item.title: 'It's a real issue' - who is the back-up to Lucy Bronze?
item.description: Alex Scott, Fara Williams, Ellen White and Steph Houghton discuss the challenge England face in finding a back-up and eventual successor to Lucy Bronze.

item.title: Young Lionesses win European trophy
item.description: England Women Under-23s have collected silverware after a 3-0 victory over Sweden in the European Competition friendly finals.

item.title: NI need to evolve and enhance in Malta - McArdle
item.description: Michael McArdle says his Northern Ireland side have to "evolve and enhance" their game in Saturday's World Cup qualifier in Malta.

item.title: Silva appeal against ban and fine partially upheld
item.description: Fulham boss Marco Silva's appeal against a one-match touchline ban and £90,000 fine is partially upheld by an independent appeal board.

item.title: He was surprise winter deadline-day signing - how is Wilson doing?
item.description: The biggest surprise of January's transfer deadline day came when Tottenham signed teenager James Wilson from Hearts. BBC Sport looks at how he is doing.

item.title: McGlynn should be 'in the conversation' to be Celtic manager - Lennon
item.description: Neil Lennon reckons John McGlynn should be "in the conversation" to become the next Celtic manager as the Dunfermline Athletic boss aims to get the better of his Falkirk counterpart in Saturday's Scottish Cup semi-final.

item.title: Spectacular European nights the new normal for Villa under Emery
item.description: Ten years ago, Aston Villa were relegated to English football's second tier for the first time since 1987. Now they are in their second European semi-final in three seasons.

item.title: Champions League in the Championship? Forest's juggling act goes on
item.description: Nottingham Forest continue their remarkable Europa League run - but does it only increase the pressure on staying in the Premier League?

item.title: Man City v Arsenal - winner takes all?
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Ben Foster and Pat Nevin discuss how the Premier League showdown between Man City and Arsenal could pan out - and what it will mean for the title race.

item.title: Farke 'proud' of 'brave' Leeds
item.description: Leeds United manager Daniel Farke says they were "brave" as they beat Manchester United 2-1 for their first league win at Old Trafford since 1981.

item.title: Tottenham players don't need a coach - De Zerbi
item.description: Tottenham manager Roberto De Zerbi says he needs to be a "big brother" and "father" figure to his players after their 1-0 defeat at Sunderland in his first game in charge leaves his side in the bottom three of the Premier League.

item.title: 'You don't want an opportunity just because you're female'
item.description: Alex Scott, Fara Williams, Ellen White and Steph Houghton debate Fifa's new ruling that every team in international women's football tournaments must have at least one female head coach or assistant coach.

item.title: De Zerbi has a lot of work to do with Spurs - Nevin
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Pat Nevin looks at how Tottenham's new head coach Roberto de Zerbi set up his side against Sunderland, and the issues that cost Spurs in their 1-0 defeat at the Stadium of Light.

item.title: Title race over if Man City don't beat Arsenal - Guardiola
item.description: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola praises Nico O'Reilly in their 3-0 win over Chelsea but says their Premier League title challenge will be over next Sunday if they fail to beat "extraordinary" Arsenal.

item.title: Who will finish 18th in the Premier League this season?
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Ben Foster and Pat Nevin have their say on which team they think are most likely to finish the season in 18th spot and be relegated from the Premier League.

item.title: How 10 Premier League teams could still qualify for Europe
item.description: Ten Premier League teams in Europe? It sounds preposterous, but it is not as far fetched as it seems.

item.title: Why Silva will be remembered as a Man City legend
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look at Bernardo Silva's glittering career at Manchester City and why fans will never forget him.

item.title: FPL talking point: How to navigate a double gameweek
item.description: The Premier League returns this weekend and, with a double gameweek, it is time to talk about chip strategies.

item.title: What is Scotland's most iconic goal? This week - Gemmill v Dalglish
item.description: Vote to find out what goal will be crowned Scotland's greatest.

item.title: The girl from Barry who grew up to be world class
item.description: Sophie Ingle is poised for another landmark in a stellar career as Wales go to Albania in Women's World Cup qualifying.

item.title: Barca say refereeing 'errors' cost them Champions League semi-final spot
item.description: Barcelona say refereeing "errors" cost them a place in the semi-finals of the Champions League, and have caused "significant sporting and financial harm to the club".

item.title: Arsenal limp to semis but Arteta wanted fire - does style matter?
item.description: Mikel Arteta wanted "fire" from his Arsenal players but instead got a sluggish performance as the Gunners did what was needed to reach the Champions League semi-finals.

item.title: Bayern links and World Cup hopes - a big summer for Gordon
item.description: Anthony Gordon faces a big summer - with World Cup hopes and the possibility that Newcastle will need to sell him. So what comes next for the England forward?

item.title: Will Iraola's summer exit speed up managerial merry-go-round?
item.description: Andoni Iraola's decision to leave Bournemouth could make it a frantic summer in the Premier League's managerial market, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

item.title: Three points off top, so why is Sutton ruling Celtic out?
item.description: BBC Scotland dissects the former Celtic striker's reasons and examines the substance behind them.

item.title: Ranking the biggest Premier League title slip-ups
item.description: With Arsenal desperate to banish their title-bottling demons, we look at the top 10 teams who have let Premier League leads slip.

item.title: 54 years, 48 leagues, 2,000 grounds - the ultimate football odyssey
item.description: BBC Sport travels with the UK's ultimate groundhopper to the final match of a decades-long, history-making journey.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: How many points do you need to stay in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks into the number of points needed to avoid relegation from the Premier League

item.title: How are the Scottish Premiership post-split fixtures decided?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership split has again been a controversial talking point, so why does it exist and why is it so complicated?

item.title: Who is one card away from suspension in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who is currently nearing a suspension in the Premier League.

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: Have all home nations ever played in same World Cup?
item.description: With England and Scotland already qualified for the 2026 World Cup, Wales and Northern Ireland both face European qualifiers in March.

item.title: All you need to know about 2027 Women's World Cup
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team take a look at everything you need to know for the 2027 Women's World Cup.

item.title: When can Arsenal win the Premier League?
item.description: Arsenal are six points clear at the top of the Premier League, but when can they officially win the title?

item.title: How rivals Falkirk & Dunfermline have turned around fortunes
item.description: When bitter rivals Falkirk and Dunfermline walk out at Hampden for Saturday's Scottish Cup semi-final it will mark an extraordinary rise for both clubs.

item.title: FC Basel postpone fixture after stadium fire
item.description: FC Basel's Swiss Super League match against FC Thun is postponed after a fire causes "significant damage" to Basel's St Jakob-Park stadium.

item.title: Spalletti signs new Juventus contract amid Italy links
item.description: Juventus manager Luciano Spalletti signs a two-year contract extension with the club, effectively ruling him out of a return to the Italy national team.

item.title: How Lens embraced underdog status to become PSG's title rivals
item.description: Lens have pushed PSG closer than anyone in Ligue 1 this season and, with the sides still to meet again, still hold an outside shot at the title.

item.title: Legendary manager Lucescu dies five days after resigning as Romania coach
item.description: Legendary manager Mircea Lucescu dies aged 80, five days after stepping down as Romania's head coach.

item.title: A big topic for others, not for her - Eta on groundbreaking head coach role
item.description: New Union Berlin head coach Marie-Louise Eta discusses becoming the first female to manage a men's team in one of Europe's top five leagues.

item.title: How Wubben-Moy has stepped into Williamson's shoes
item.description: England defender Lotte Wubben-Moy took her chance when she was handed a rare start in Tuesday's victory over Spain in Leah Williamson's absence.

item.title: Success over Spain - but how can England get best out of classy James?
item.description: How can Sarina Wiegman get the best from one of England's most talented footballers, Lauren James?

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
